initial_prompt:
Super-Eywa v1 runtime prompt snapshot.
Turn index: 1
Node depth: 0
Allowed decisions: ['delegate']
Prompt family: delegate
Base header prompt: ''
Selected prompt text:
Decompose the problem into a maximum of 2 distinct, functional subproblems for other agents.

Each helper must be assigned one focused, actionable mathematical or algorithmic task that materially contributes to the final solution. Do not restate the original question. Use plain text only inside helper instructions (no markdown, code fences, or LaTeX).

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "delegate",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "helpers": [
    {
      "label": "helper_1",
      "instructions": "plain-text subproblem for the first helper"
    }
  ],
  "synthesis_brief": "how the parent should combine the child results"
}
Additional instruction prompt profiles: []
System prompt:

Each helper must be assigned one focused, actionable mathematical or algorithmic task that materially contributes to the final solution. Do not restate the original question. Use plain text only inside helper instructions (no markdown, code fences, or LaTeX).

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:

Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-S4-ahc030-polyomino-mining
Title: S4. AHC030 / Polyomino Mining

Problem:
Given known polyomino oil-field shapes but unknown placements, recover all oil-containing cells with as little probing cost as possible using drilling, noisy aggregate queries, and final guesses.

Grading target:
Lower probing cost is better.
Contest score is derived from absolute and relative cost.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
final_prompt:
Super-Eywa v1 runtime prompt snapshot.
Turn index: 2
Node depth: 0
Allowed decisions: ['execute_locally']
Prompt family: delegate
Base header prompt: ''
Selected prompt text:
Child results have already come back to you. Use them to produce the final response yourself. Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "string",
  "result_type": "optional string"
}
Additional instruction prompt profiles: []
System prompt:


User prompt:
Child results have already come back to you. Use them to produce the final response yourself. Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "string",
  "result_type": "optional string"
}

Synthesis brief from the earlier child-creation step:
The parent agent will use the probabilistic field estimator to maintain a belief map of the oil locations. The search strategy optimizer will then use this belief map to select the most cost-effective next action (drilling or querying) until the uncertainty is low enough to make a final guess with minimal cost.

Child results already returned to you:
[
  {
    "node_id": "node_root_helper_01",
    "summary": "FINAL_ANSWER: Greedy heuristic using Bayesian information gain\nJUSTIFICATION: Maximizes information gain per query cost using Bayesian updates."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "node_root_helper_02",
    "summary": "FINAL_ANSWER: P(S|Q, D) proportional to P(Q|S) * P(S|D)\nJUSTIFICATION: Bayesian update using geometric shape constraints and noisy data."
  }
]
